target.name: Archetype AI
target.founder: Ivan Poupyrev, Brandon Barbello, Leonardo Giusti, Jaime Lien, Nicholas Gillian

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $13M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $8.3B - $25.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Archetype AI is a Seed-stage AI & Software company that has raised $13M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Reflection AI ($25.0B Series A), Applied Intuition ($15.0B Series A), and Together AI ($8.3B Series A). The broader comp universe includes 1 public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and 8 private peers spanning frontier AI infrastructure.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $13M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying $32M. Peer-median analysis derives a $15.0B median valuation across the three identified comps, reflecting the substantial capital deployed and stage positioning of comparable firms. The peer range spans $8.3B to $25.0B.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a low bound of $28M, a median of $7.5B, and a high bound of $17.2B. The wide dispersion reflects fundamental uncertainty about stage-appropriate valuation: the stage-progression method anchors to deployed capital and near-term step-up mechanics, while peer-median analysis captures later-stage capital intensity and market positioning in a high-growth sector. Resolution of this range depends critically on revenue traction, technology differentiation, and pathway to next fundraise.
